select-all.label: Selecting All Columns
select-all.summary: Use the wildcard (*) to select all columns.

sketch-what-is-select-all.title: What Does SELECT * Mean?
sketch-what-is-select-all.bodyMarkdown: In SQL, you can use the asterisk (*) as a wildcard to select all columns from a table. This is useful when you want to see every column for each row in your results, without having to list each column name individually.

sketch-when-to-use-select-all.title: When Should You Use SELECT *?
sketch-when-to-use-select-all.bodyMarkdown: SELECT * is handy for quick exploration or when you need every piece of data from a table. However, in production queries, it's often better to specify only the columns you need for performance and clarity.

quiz-single-choice-select-all-syntax.title: Syntax for Selecting All Columns
quiz-single-choice-select-all-syntax.prompt: Which SQL statement selects all columns from the table named 'products'?

help.concept: The asterisk (*) is used in SQL to represent all columns in a table.
help.hint_1: The correct statement uses * after SELECT.
help.hint_2: The statement should read: SELECT * FROM table_name;

quiz-multi-choice-select-all-effects.title: Effects of SELECT *
quiz-multi-choice-select-all-effects.prompt: What happens when you use SELECT * in a query? (Choose all that apply.)
quiz-multi-choice-select-all-effects.hint: Think about what * means and what data is returned.

help.concept: SELECT * returns every column for each row in the table, which may include unnecessary data.
help.hint_1: You don't have to specify columns with *.
help.hint_2: SELECT * can result in large result sets if the table has many columns.

options.a: All columns from the table are included in the result.
options.b: Only the first column is selected.
options.c: You don't need to list column names.
options.d: It can return more data than needed.

quiz-drag-reorder-select-all-steps.title: Ordering a SELECT * Query
quiz-drag-reorder-select-all-steps.prompt: Arrange the parts of a SQL query to select all columns from the 'students' table in the correct order.
quiz-drag-reorder-select-all-steps.hint: Start with the SQL command, then the wildcard, then specify the table.

help.concept: A basic SQL SELECT statement follows the order: SELECT, columns (or *), FROM, table name, semicolon.
help.hint_2: FROM and the table name come after SELECT *.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-all-keywor.title: Fill in the Blank: SELECT *
quiz-fill-blank-choice-select-all-keywor.prompt: Fill in the blank to select all columns from the 'employees' table: SELECT ___ FROM employees;

help.concept: The asterisk (*) is the wildcard character for all columns in SQL.
help.hint_1: It's not a word, but a symbol.
help.hint_2: It's the same symbol used for multiplication.

quiz-code-input-select-all-employees.title: Write a SELECT * Query
quiz-code-input-select-all-employees.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the 'employees' table.
quiz-code-input-select-all-employees.hint: Use SELECT, the wildcard, and FROM.

help.concept: To select all columns, use SELECT * followed by FROM and the table name.
